title: Should source-region classification become one shared soft model?
summary: Several current modules still answer comment, literal, declaration, and rendered-width questions through separate Tree-sitter queries, text scans, and window matches.
details: util provides shared comment, static_assert, clang-format-off, literal, and buffer-line caches, but pointer.lua retains a private naive string/comment scan and markers still combines Tree-sitter-gated prefix extmarks with regex color masks.
parse/render/view and raw-line modules sometimes derive overlapping structure independently, creating composition risk such as INC-002.
A bounded visible-region model could unify only the facts multiple consumers need; it must not become a C++ compiler or duplicate clangd.
The decision requires measurements and parity tests per migrated consumer; the existence of duplicated logic alone does not prove a new model is cheaper or safer.

provenance.note: Current duplication and ownership boundaries were verified directly from the implementation.

evidence.claim: Multiple current classification mechanisms remain observable in code.

questions: Is a shared soft-model currently approved?
What would constrain a shared source-region model?

last_verified.method: Fresh code comparison of current classifiers; intentionally left open.
